Defaults to all scopes if not specified. */\n  scope?: OptInFeatureScope[];\n}\n\n/** All available scopes for feature opt-in configuration */\nexport const ALL_SCOPES: OptInFeatureScope[] = [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"];\n\n/**\n * Features that appear in opt-in settings.\n * Add new features here to make them available for user/team opt-in.\n */\nexport const OPT_IN_FEATURES: OptInFeatureConfig[] = [\n  // Example - to be populated with actual features\n  // {\n  //   slug: \"bookings-v3\",\n  //   titleI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_title\",\n  //   descriptionI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_description\",\n the opt-in allowlist.\n * Acts as a type guard, narrowing the slug to FeatureId when true.\n */\nexport function isOptInFeature(slug: string): slug is FeatureId {\n  return OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => f.slug === slug);\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if there are any opt-in features available.\n */\nexport const HAS_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.length > 0;\n\n/** Whether there are opt-in features available for the user scope */\nexport const HAS_USER_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"user\"));\n\n/** Whether there are opt-in features available for the team scope */\nexport const HAS_TEAM_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"team\"));\n\n/** Whether there are opt-in features available for the org scope */\nexport const HAS_ORG_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"org\"));\n\n/**\n * Get opt-in features that are available for a specific scope.\n * Features without a scope field are available for all scopes.\n */\nexport function getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope: OptInFeatureScope): OptInFeatureConfig[] {\n  return OPT_IN_FEATURES.filter((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(scope));\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a feature is allowed for a specific scope.\n * Features without a scope field are allowed for all scopes.\n * Features not in the config are NOT allowed (must be explicitly configured).\n */\nexport function isFeatureAllowedForScope(slug: string, scope: OptInFeatureScope): boolean {\n  const config = getOptInFeatureConfig(slug);\n  if (!config) return false;\n  return config.scope.includes(scope);\n}\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BtE2r3ZUZSqXR2UabWRUXh","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { FeatureId, FeatureState } from \"@calcom/features/flags/config\";\n     2→import type { FeaturesRepository } from \"@calcom/features/flags/features.repository\";\n     3→import { ErrorCode } from \"@calcom/lib/errorCodes\";\n     4→import { ErrorWithCode } from \"@calcom/lib/errors\";\n     5→\n     6→import { getOptInFeatureConfig, getOptInFeaturesForScope, isFeatureAllowedForScope } from \"../config\";\n     7→import { applyAutoOptIn } from \"../lib/applyAutoOptIn\";\n     8→import { computeEffectiveStateAcrossTeams } from \"../lib/computeEffectiveState\";\n     9→import type { OptInFeaturePolicy, OptInFeatureScope } from \"../types\";\n    10→import type {\n    11→  EffectiveStateReason,\n    12→  IFeatureOptInService,\n    13→  ResolvedFeatureState,\n    14→} from \"./IFeatureOptInService\";\n    15→\n    16→type ListFeaturesForUserResult = {\n    17→  featureId: FeatureId;\n    18→  globalEnabled: boolean;\n    19→  orgState: FeatureState;\n    20→  teamStates: FeatureState[];\n    21→  userState: FeatureState | undefined;\n    22→  effectiveEnabled: boolean;\n    23→  effectiveReason: EffectiveStateReason;\n    24→  orgAutoOptIn: boolean;\n    25→  teamAutoOptIns: boolean[];\n    26→  userAutoOptIn: boolean;\n    27→};\n    28→\n    29→type ListFeaturesForTeamResult = {\n    30→  featureId: FeatureId;\n    31→  globalEnabled: boolean;\n    32→  teamState: FeatureState;\n    33→  orgState: FeatureState;\n    34→};\n    35→\n    36→function getOrgState(orgId: number | null, teamStatesById: Record<number, FeatureState>): FeatureState {\n    37→  if (orgId !== null) {\n    38→    return teamStatesById[orgId] ?? \"inherit\";\n    39→  }\n    40→  return \"inherit\";\n    41→}\n    42→\n    43→function getOrgAutoOptIn(orgId: number | null, teamsAutoOptIn: Record<number, boolean>): boolean {\n    44→  if (orgId !== null) {\n    45→    return teamsAutoOptIn[orgId] ?? false;\n    46→  }\n    47→  return false;\n    48→}\n    49→\n    50→function getTeamIdsToQuery(teamId: number, parentOrgId: number | null | undefined): number[] {\n    51→  if (parentOrgId) {\n    52→    return [teamId, parentOrgId];\n    53→  }\n    54→  return [teamId];\n    55→}\n    56→\n    57→function getOrgStateForTeam(\n    58→  parentOrgId: number | null | undefined,\n    59→  teamStates: Record<string, Record<number, FeatureState>>,\n    60→  slug: FeatureId\n    61→): FeatureState {\n    62→  if (parentOrgId) {\n    63→    return teamStates[slug]?.[parentOrgId] ?? \"inherit\";\n    64→  }\n    65→  return \"inherit\";\n    66→}\n    67→\n    68→/**\n    69→ * Service class for managing feature opt-in logic.\n    70→ * Computes effective states based on global, org, team, and user settings.\n    71→ */\n    72→export class FeatureOptInService implements IFeatureOptInService {\n    73→  constructor(private featuresRepository: FeaturesRepository) {}\n    74→\n    75→  /**\n    76→   * Core method: Resolve feature states for a user across all their teams.\n    77→   *\n    78→   * Precedence rules:\n    79→   * 1. Global disabled → false (stop)\n    80→   * 2. Org explicitly disabled → false (stop)\n    81→   * 3. Org explicitly enabled → allowed at org level, continue to teams\n    82→   * 4. Org inherits (or no org) → check teams\n    83→   * 5. All teams explicitly disabled → false (stop)\n    84→   * 6. At least one team enabled OR inherits → allowed at team level, continue to user\n    85→   * 7. User explicitly disabled → false\n    86→   * 8. User explicitly enabled OR inherits → true\n    87→   *\n    88→   * Auto-opt-in transformation:\n    89→   * - If autoOptInFeatures=true at a level AND state is \"inherit\", transform to \"enabled\"\n    90→   * - This transformation happens before computing effectiveEnabled\n    91→   */\n    92→  async resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams({\n    93→    userId,\n    94→    orgId,\n    95→    teamIds,\n    96→    featureIds,\n    97→  }: {\n    98→    userId: number;\n    99→    orgId: number | null;\n   100→    teamIds: number[];\n   101→    featureIds: FeatureId[];\n   102→  }): Promise<Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState>> {\n   103→    const allTeamIds = orgId !== null ? [orgId, ...teamIds] : teamIds;\n   104→\n   105→    const [allFeatures, allTeamStates, userStates, userAutoOptIn, teamsAutoOptIn] = await Promise.all([\n   106→      this.featuresRepository.getAllFeatures(),\n   107→      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsFeatureStates({ teamIds: allTeamIds, featureIds }),\n   108→      this.featuresRepository.getUserFeatureStates({ userId, featureIds }),\n   109→      this.featuresRepository.getUserAutoOptIn(userId),\n   110→      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsAutoOptIn(allTeamIds),\n   111→    ]);\n   112→\n   113→    const globalEnabledMap = new Map(allFeatures.map((feature) => [feature.slug, feature.enabled ?? false]));\n   114→    const resolvedStates: Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState> = {};\n   115→\n   116→    for (const featureId of featureIds) {\n   117→      const state = this.resolveFeatureState(\n   118→        featureId,\n   119→        orgId,\n   120→        teamIds,\n   121→        globalEnabledMap,\n   122→        allTeamStates,\n   123→        userStates,\n   124→        teamsAutoOptIn,\n   125→        userAutoOptIn\n   126→      );\n   127→      resolvedStates[featureId] = state;\n   128→    }\n   129→\n   130→    return resolvedStates;\n   131→  }\n   132→\n   133→  private resolveFeatureState(\n   134→    featureId: FeatureId,\n   135→    orgId: number | null,\n   136→    teamIds: number[],\n   137→    globalEnabledMap: Map<string, boolean>,\n   138→    allTeamStates: Record<string, Record<number, FeatureState>>,\n   139→    userStates: Record<string, FeatureState>,\n   140→    teamsAutoOptIn: Record<number, boolean>,\n   141→    userAutoOptIn: boolean\n   142→  ): ResolvedFeatureState {\n   143→    const globalEnabled = globalEnabledMap.get(featureId) ?? false;\n   144→    const teamStatesById = allTeamStates[featureId] ?? {};\n   145→\n   146→    const orgState = getOrgState(orgId, teamStatesById);\n   147→    const teamStates = teamIds.map((teamId) => teamStatesById[teamId] ?? \"inherit\");\n   148→    const userState = userStates[featureId] ?? \"inherit\";\n   149→    const orgAutoOptIn = getOrgAutoOptIn(orgId, teamsAutoOptIn);\n   150→    const teamAutoOptIns = teamIds.map((teamId) => teamsAutoOptIn[teamId] ?? false);\n   151→\n   152→    const { effectiveOrgState, effectiveTeamStates, effectiveUserState } = applyAutoOptIn({\n   153→      orgState,\n   154→      teamStates,\n   155→      userState,\n   156→      orgAutoOptIn,\n   157→      teamAutoOptIns,\n   158→      userAutoOptIn,\n   159→    });\n   160→\n   161→    const policy: OptInFeaturePolicy = \"permissive\";\n   162→\n   163→    const { enabled: effectiveEnabled, reason: effectiveReason } = computeEffectiveStateAcrossTeams({\n   164→      globalEnabled,\n   165→      orgState: effectiveOrgState,\n   166→      teamStates: effectiveTeamStates,\n   167→      userState: effectiveUserState,\n   168→      policy,\n   169→    });\n   170→\n   171→    return {\n   172→      featureId,\n   173→      globalEnabled,\n   174→      orgState,\n   175→      teamStates,\n   176→      userState,\n   177→      effectiveEnabled,\n   178→      effectiveReason,\n   179→      orgAutoOptIn,\n   180→      teamAutoOptIns,\n   181→      userAutoOptIn,\n   182→    };\n   183→  }\n   184→\n   185→  /**\n   186→   * List all opt-in features with their states for a user across teams.\n   187→   * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to \"user\".\n   188→   */\n   189→  async listFeaturesForUser(input: {\n   190→    userId: number;\n   191→    orgId: number | null;\n   192→    teamIds: number[];\n   193→  }): Promise<ListFeaturesForUserResult[]> {\n   194→    const { userId, orgId, teamIds } = input;\n   195→    const userScopedFeatures = getOptInFeaturesForScope(\"user\");\n   196→    const featureIds = userScopedFeatures.map((config) => config.slug);\n   197→\n   198→    const resolvedStates = await this.resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams({\n   199→      userId,\n   200→      orgId,\n   201→      teamIds,\n   202→      featureIds,\n   203→    });\n   204→\n   205→    return featureIds.map((featureId) => resolvedStates[featureId]).filter((state) => !state.globalEnabled);\n   206→  }\n   207→\n   208→  /**\n   209→   * List all opt-in features with their raw states for a team or organization.\n   210→   * Used for team/org admin settings page to configure feature opt-in.\n   211→   * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to the specified scope.\n   212→   * If parentOrgId is provided, also returns the organization state for each feature.\n   213→   */\n   214→  async listFeaturesForTeam(input: {\n   215→    teamId: number;\n   216→    parentOrgId?: number | null;\n   217→    scope?: OptInFeatureScope;\n   218→  }): Promise<ListFeaturesForTeamResult[]> {\n   219→    const { teamId, parentOrgId, scope = \"team\" } = input;\n   220→    const teamIdsToQuery = getTeamIdsToQuery(teamId, parentOrgId);\n   221→    const scopedFeatures = getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope);\n   222→\n   223→    const [allFeatures, teamStates] = await Promise.all([\n   224→      this.featuresRepository.getAllFeatures(),\n   225→      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsFeatureStates({\n   226→        teamIds: teamIdsToQuery,\n   227→        featureIds: scopedFeatures.map((config) => config.slug),\n   228→      }),\n   229→    ]);\n   230→\n   231→    const results = scopedFeatures.map((config) => {\n   232→      const globalFeature = allFeatures.find((f) => f.slug === config.slug);\n   233→      const globalEnabled = globalFeature?.enabled ?? false;\n   234→      const teamState = teamStates[config.slug]?.[teamId] ?? \"inherit\";\n   235→      const orgState = getOrgStateForTeam(parentOrgId, teamStates, config.slug);\n   236→\n   237→      return { featureId: config.slug, globalEnabled, teamState, orgState };\n   238→    });\n   239→\n   240→    return results.filter((result) => result.globalEnabled);\n   241→  }\n   242→\n   243→  /**\n   244→   * Set user's feature state.\n   245→   * Delegates to FeaturesRepository.setUserFeatureState.\n   246→   * Throws an error if the feature is not scoped to \"user\".\n   247→   */\n   248→  async setUserFeatureState(\n   249→    input:\n   250→      | { userId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"enabled\" | \"disabled\"; assignedBy: number }\n   251→      | { userId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"inherit\" }\n   252→  ): Promise<void> {\n   253→    const { userId, featureId, state } = input;\n   254→\n   255→    if (!isFeatureAllowedForScope(featureId, \"user\")) {\n   256→      throw new ErrorWithCode(\n   257→        ErrorCode.BadRequest,\n   258→        `Feature \"${featureId}\" is not available at the user scope`\n   259→      );\n   260→    }\n   261→\n   262→    if (state === \"inherit\") {\n   263→      await this.featuresRepository.setUserFeatureState({ userId, featureId, state });\n   264→    } else {\n   265→      const { assignedBy } = input;\n   266→      await this.featuresRepository.setUserFeatureState({\n   267→        userId,\n   268→        featureId,\n   269→        state,\n   270→        assignedBy: `user-${assignedBy}`,\n   271→      });\n   272→    }\n   273→  }\n   274→\n   275→  /**\n   276→   * Set team's feature state.\n   277→   * Delegates to FeaturesRepository.setTeamFeatureState.\n   278→   * Throws an error if the feature is not scoped \"inherit\";\n  }\n  return \"inherit\";\n}\n\nfunction getOrgAutoOptIn(orgId: number | null, teamsAutoOptIn: Record<number, boolean>): boolean {\n  if (orgId !== null) {\n    return teamsAutoOptIn[orgId] ?? false;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nfunction getTeamIdsToQuery(teamId: number, parentOrgId: number | null | undefined): number[] {\n  if (parentOrgId) {\n    return [teamId, parentOrgId];\n  }\n  return [teamId];\n}\n\nfunction getOrgStateForTeam(\n  parentOrgId: number | null | undefined,\n  teamStates: Record<string, Record<number, FeatureState>>,\n  slug: FeatureId\n): FeatureState {\n  if (parentOrgId) {\n    return teamStates[slug]?.[parentOrgId] ?? \"inherit\";\n  }\n  return \"inherit\";\n}\n\n/**\n * Service class for managing feature opt-in logic.\n * Computes effective states based on global, org, team, and user settings.\n */\nexport class FeatureOptInService implements IFeatureOptInService {\n  constructor(private featuresRepository: FeaturesRepository) {}\n\n  /**\n   * Core method: Resolve feature states for a user across all their teams.\n   *\n   * Precedence rules:\n   * 1. Global disabled → false (stop)\n   * 2. Org explicitly disabled → false (stop)\n   * 3. Org explicitly enabled → allowed at org level, continue to teams\n   * 4. Org inherits (or no org) → check teams\n   * 5. All teams explicitly disabled → false (stop)\n   * 6. At least one team enabled OR inherits → allowed at team level, continue to user\n   * 7. User explicitly disabled → false\n   * 8. User explicitly enabled OR inherits → true\n   *\n   * Auto-opt-in transformation:\n   * - If autoOptInFeatures=true at a level AND state is \"inherit\", transform to \"enabled\"\n   * - This transformation happens before computing effectiveEnabled\n   */\n  async resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams({\n    userId,\n    orgId,\n    teamIds,\n    featureIds,\n  }: {\n    userId: number;\n    orgId: number | null;\n    teamIds: number[];\n    featureIds: FeatureId[];\n  }): Promise<Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState>> {\n    const allTeamIds = orgId !== null ? [orgId, ...teamIds] : teamIds;\n\n    const [allFeatures, allTeamStates, userStates, userAutoOptIn, teamsAutoOptIn] = await Promise.all([\n      this.featuresRepository.getAllFeatures(),\n      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsFeatureStates({ teamIds: allTeamIds, featureIds }),\n      this.featuresRepository.getUserFeatureStates({ userId, featureIds }),\n      this.featuresRepository.getUserAutoOptIn(userId),\n      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsAutoOptIn(allTeamIds),\n    ]);\n\n    const globalEnabledMap = new Map(allFeatures.map((feature) => [feature.slug, feature.enabled ?? false]));\n    const resolvedStates: Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState> = {};\n\n    for (const featureId of featureIds) {\n      const state = this.resolveFeatureState(\n        featureId,\n        orgId,\n        teamIds,\n        globalEnabledMap,\n        allTeamStates,\n        userStates,\n        teamsAutoOptIn,\n        userAutoOptIn\n      );\n      resolvedStates[featureId] = state;\n    }\n\n    return resolvedStates;\n  }\n\n  private resolveFeatureState(\n    featureId: FeatureId,\n    orgId: number | null,\n    teamIds: number[],\n    globalEnabledMap: Map<string, boolean>,\n    allTeamStates: Record<string, Record<number, FeatureState>>,\n    userStates: Record<string, FeatureState>,\n    teamsAutoOptIn: Record<number, boolean>,\n    userAutoOptIn: boolean\n  ): ResolvedFeatureState {\n    const globalEnabled = globalEnabledMap.get(featureId) ?? false;\n    const teamStatesById = allTeamStates[featureId] ?? {};\n\n    const orgState = getOrgState(orgId, teamStatesById);\n    const teamStates = teamIds.map((teamId) => teamStatesById[teamId] ?? \"inherit\");\n    const userState = userStates[featureId] ?? \"inherit\";\n    const orgAutoOptIn = getOrgAutoOptIn(orgId, teamsAutoOptIn);\n    const teamAutoOptIns = teamIds.map((teamId) => teamsAutoOptIn[teamId] ?? false);\n\n    const { effectiveOrgState, effectiveTeamStates, effectiveUserState } = applyAutoOptIn({\n      orgState,\n      teamStates,\n      userState,\n      orgAutoOptIn,\n      teamAutoOptIns,\n      userAutoOptIn,\n    });\n\n    const policy: OptInFeaturePolicy = \"permissive\";\n\n    const { enabled: effectiveEnabled, reason: effectiveReason } = computeEffectiveStateAcrossTeams({\n      globalEnabled,\n      orgState: effectiveOrgState,\n      teamStates: effectiveTeamStates,\n      userState: effectiveUserState,\n      policy,\n    });\n\n    return {\n      featureId,\n      globalEnabled,\n      orgState,\n      teamStates,\n      userState,\n      effectiveEnabled,\n      effectiveReason,\n      orgAutoOptIn,\n      teamAutoOptIns,\n      userAutoOptIn,\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * List all opt-in features with their states for a user across teams.\n   * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to \"user\".\n   */\n  async listFeaturesForUser(input: {\n    userId: number;\n    orgId: number | null;\n    teamIds: number[];\n  }): Promise<ListFeaturesForUserResult[]> {\n    const { userId, orgId, teamIds } = input;\n    const userScopedFeatures = getOptInFeaturesForScope(\"user\");\n    const featureIds = userScopedFeatures.map((config) => config.slug);\n\n    const resolvedStates = await this.resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams({\n      userId,\n      orgId,\n      teamIds,\n      featureIds,\n    });\n\n    return featureIds.map((featureId) => resolvedStates[featureId]).filter((state) => !state.globalEnabled);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * List all opt-in features with their raw states for a team or organization.\n   * Used for team/org admin settings page to configure feature opt-in.\n   * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to the specified scope.\n   * If parentOrgId is provided, also returns the organization state for each feature.\n   */\n  async listFeaturesForTeam(input: {\n    teamId: number;\n    parentOrgId?: number | null;\n    scope?: OptInFeatureScope;\n  }): Promise<ListFeaturesForTeamResult[]> {\n    const { teamId, parentOrgId, scope = \"team\" } = input;\n    const teamIdsToQuery = getTeamIdsToQuery(teamId, parentOrgId);\n    const scopedFeatures = getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope);\n\n    const [allFeatures, teamStates] = await Promise.all([\n      this.featuresRepository.getAllFeatures(),\n      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsFeatureStates({\n        teamIds: teamIdsToQuery,\n        featureIds: scopedFeatures.map((config) => config.slug),\n      }),\n    ]);\n\n    const results = scopedFeatures.map((config) => {\n      const globalFeature = allFeatures.find((f) => f.slug === config.slug);\n      const globalEnabled = globalFeature?.enabled ?? false;\n      const teamState = teamStates[config.slug]?.[teamId] ?? \"inherit\";\n      const orgState = getOrgStateForTeam(parentOrgId, teamStates, config.slug);\n\n      return { featureId: config.slug, globalEnabled, teamState, orgState };\n    });\n\n    return results.filter((result) => result.globalEnabled);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Set user's feature state.\n   * Delegates to FeaturesRepository.setUserFeatureState.\n   * Throws an error if the feature is not scoped to \"user\".\n   */\n  async setUserFeatureState(\n    input:\n      | { userId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"enabled\" | \"disabled\"; assignedBy: number }\n      | { userId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"inherit\" }\n  ): Promise<void> {\n    const { userId, featureId, state } = input;\n\n    if (!isFeatureAllowedForScope(featureId, \"user\")) {\n      throw new ErrorWithCode(\n        ErrorCode.BadRequest,\n        `Feature \"${featureId}\" is not available at the user scope`\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (state === \"inherit\") {\n      await this.featuresRepository.setUserFeatureState({ userId, featureId, state });\n    } else {\n      const { assignedBy } = input;\n      await this.featuresRepository.setUserFeatureState({\n        userId,\n        featureId,\n        state,\n        assignedBy: `user-${assignedBy}`,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Set team's feature state.\n   * Delegates to FeaturesRepository.setTeamFeatureState.\n   * Throws an error if the feature is not scoped to the specified scope.\n   */\n  async setTeamFeatureState(\n    input:\n      | { teamId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"enabled\" | \"disabled\"; assignedBy: number; scope?: OptInFeatureScope }\n      | { teamId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"inherit\"; scope?: OptInFeatureScope }\n  ): Promise<void> {\n    const { teamId, featureId, state } = input;\n    const scope = input.scope ?? \"team\";\n\n    if (!isFeatureAllowedForScope(featureId, scope)) {\n      throw new ErrorWithCode(\n        ErrorCode.BadRequest,\n        `Feature \"${featureId}\" is not available at the ${scope} scope`\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (state === \"inherit\") {\n      await this.featuresRepository.setTeamFeatureState({ teamId, featureId, state });\n    } else {\n      const { assignedBy } = input;\n      await this.featuresRepository.setTeamFeatureState({\n        teamId,\n        featureId,\n        state,\n        assignedBy: `user-${assignedBy}`,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n}\n","numLines":308,"startLine":1,"totalLines":308}}}

Let me check if there's a function to get the policy from the config...\n\n\nThe `resolveFeatureState` method is a private method called from `resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams`, which has access to `featureIds`. The policy should be retrieved from `getOptInFeatureConfig(featureId)?.policy ?? \"permissive\"` instead of being hardcoded for all features. This ensures each feature uses its configured policy rather than defaulting everything to permissive. config, not be hardcoded. The `getOptInFeatureConfig` function is available and should be used to retrieve the policy for each feature ID instead of using a constant value.\n\nThere's also an issue at line 205 in `listFeaturesForUser` where the filter logic contradicts the comment. The comment indicates features should be globally enabled, but the code filters for `!state.globalEnabled`, which selects the opposite set of features.\n\nLooking at the broader context, opt-in features are typically disabled by default and require explicit user enrollment, so filtering for non-globally-enabled features makes sense semantically. However, this creates an inconsistency with `listFeaturesForTeam` at line 210, which filters for `result.globalEnabled` instead. The two functions are using opposite filter conditions, suggesting either the comment is inaccurate or there's a logical error in one of the implementations. I need to reconsider what \"opt-in feature\" actually means. If a feature is disabled by default, users must explicitly enable it—that would make it an opt-in feature, suggesting the filter should check for `!globalEnabled`. However, `listFeaturesForTeam` at line 240 filters for `globalEnabled` instead, which creates another inconsistency to investigate.\n\nLooking at `listFeaturesForUser`, it retrieves user-scoped features and then filters the results to exclude globally enabled ones. This aligns with the opt-in pattern. The `listFeaturesForTeam` function appears to follow a different filtering logic that I need to examine more carefully.\n\nFor the team listing, it determines which team IDs to query based on the input, fetches the opt-in features for the specified scope, and then retrieves both all available features and the team's feature states in parallel. It then maps through the scoped features to find matching global feature data.\n\nThe function returns only features that are globally enabled, which differs from the user listing function that doesn't apply this filter. This inconsistency should be resolved—both functions should handle filtering the same way since they serve similar purposes for different entity types.\n\nLooking at the comments more carefully, line 187-188 explicitly states the function returns features that are \"globally enabled,\" meaning `globalEnabled === true`. But line 205 uses `.filter((state) => !state.globalEnabled)`, which is the opposite. This is a clear bug where the filter logic needs to be inverted to match the documented behavior.\n\nHowever, reconsidering from a product angle: opt-in features are typically disabled by default (not globally enabled) and require explicit user activation. If that's the intended design, then `listFeaturesForTeam` on line 240 filtering for `globalEnabled` would be inconsistent. The most likely scenario is both functions should filter for `!globalEnabled` to return opt-in features, meaning line 240 also contains a bug.\n\nLooking more carefully at the comment on line 211 stating \"globally enabled\" creates further confusion—that phrasing contradicts what opt-in features should be. Examining the actual code behavior versus the documented intent reveals the core inconsistency: `listFeaturesForUser` and `listFeaturesForTeam` should both return the same category of features (those not globally enabled), yet they're filtering in opposite directions.\n\nThe comment for `listFeaturesForUser` claims it returns \"globally enabled\" features, but the filter explicitly checks for `!state.globalEnabled`. Meanwhile, `listFeaturesForTeam` has matching comment and filter—both reference \"globally enabled\" and filter for `result.globalEnabled`. This asymmetry suggests line 205 needs to flip to `.filter((state) => state.globalEnabled)` to align with the documented behavior and the pattern in `listFeaturesForTeam`.\n\nThe intent appears to be that \"globally enabled\" means the feature is available system-wide, allowing users and teams to opt in or out individually. Both functions should therefore filter for features where `globalEnabled === true`. So line 205 should be corrected to filter for `state.globalEnabled` rather than its negation.\n\nMoving to the router file, the `setUserState` endpoint doesn't appear to validate the slug against the allowlist.  The `setTeamState` and `setOrganizationState` endpoints both include validation that throws an error if the slug isn't an opt-in feature, but `setUserState` is missing this check entirely. This is a security concern since user inputs should be validated against the allowlist before being processed. The `isOptInFeature` type guard narrows the input to the correct type, so once validation passes, the type is properly constrained for the service call. **Severity**: High\n   - **Issue**: Documentation says \"globally enabled\" but code filters for `!globalEnabled`\n   - **Fix**: Invert filter to `state.globalEnabled` (consistent with `listFeaturesForTeam`)\n\n4. **_router.ts:95** — Missing slug validation in `setUserState` endpoint\n   - **Severity**: High\n   - **Issue**: `setUserState` lacks validation present in `setTeamState`/`setOrganizationState`\n   - **Fix**: Add `isOptInFeature` check before service call\n\nAll proposed fixes are provided as unified diffs in the JSON report and are ready for application."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2386,"cache_read_input_tokens":36636,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2386},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c707fffd-963c-4eb8-87b6-4561afdd0f45","uuid":"88778aa7-6d3b-497f-a857-78dadf9c378f"}
